modules.label: vmware_category

fields.label: associable object types
fields.description: List of object types that can be associated with the given category.

fields.label: category cardinality
fields.description: The category cardinality. This parameter is ignored, when updating existing category. V(single) means an object can only be assigned one of the tags in this category. For example, if a O(category_name=Operating System), then different tags of this category would be "Windows", "Linux", and so on. In this case a VM object can be assigned only one of these tags and hence the cardinality of the associated category here is V(single). V(multiple) means an object can be assigned several of the tags in this category. For example, if a O(category_name=Server), then different tags of this category would be "AppServer", "DatabaseServer" and so on. In this case a VM object can be assigned more than one of the above tags and hence the cardinality of the associated category here is V(multiple).

modules.label: vmware_cluster_ha
modules.tooltip: Manage High Availability (HA) on VMware vSphere clusters

fields.label: advanced settings
fields.description: A dictionary of advanced HA settings.

fields.label: apd delay
fields.description: The response recovery delay time in sec for storage failures categorized as All Paths Down (APD). Only set if O(apd_response=restartConservative) or O(apd_response=restartAggressive).

fields.label: apd reaction
fields.description: VM response recovery reaction for storage failures categorized as All Paths Down (APD). Only set if O(apd_response=restartConservative) or O(apd_response=restartAggressive).

fields.label: apd response
fields.description: VM storage protection setting for storage failures categorized as All Paths Down (APD).

fields.label: cluster name
fields.description: The name of the cluster to be managed.

fields.label: datacenter
fields.description: The name of the datacenter.

fields.label: enable
fields.description: Whether to enable HA.

fields.label: ha host monitoring
fields.description: Whether HA restarts virtual machines after a host fails. If set to V(enabled), HA restarts virtual machines after a host fails. If set to V(disabled), HA does not restart virtual machines after a host fails. If O(enable=false), this value is ignored.

fields.label: ha restart priority
fields.description: Priority HA gives to a virtual machine if sufficient capacity is not available to power on all failed virtual machines. Valid only when O(ha_vm_monitoring=vmAndAppMonitoring) or O(ha_vm_monitoring=vmMonitoringOnly). If set to V(disabled), then HA is disabled for this virtual machine. If set to V(high), then virtual machine with this priority have a higher chance of powering on after a failure, when there is insufficient capacity on hosts to meet all virtual machine needs. If set to V(medium), then virtual machine with this priority have an intermediate chance of powering on after a failure, when there is insufficient capacity on hosts to meet all virtual machine needs. If set to V(low), then virtual machine with this priority have a lower chance of powering on after a failure, when there is insufficient capacity on hosts to meet all virtual machine needs.

fields.label: ha vm failure interval
fields.description: The number of seconds after which virtual machine is declared as failed if no heartbeat has been received. This setting is only valid if O(ha_vm_monitoring=vmAndAppMonitoring) or O(ha_vm_monitoring=vmMonitoringOnly). Unit is seconds.

fields.label: ha vm max failure window
fields.description: The number of seconds for the window during which up to O(ha_vm_max_failures) resets can occur before automated responses stop. Valid only when O(ha_vm_monitoring=vmAndAppMonitoring) or O(ha_vm_monitoring=vmMonitoringOnly). Unit is seconds. Default specifies no failure window.

fields.label: ha vm max failures
fields.description: Maximum number of failures and automated resets allowed during the time that O(ha_vm_max_failure_window) specifies. Valid only when O(ha_vm_monitoring=vmAndAppMonitoring) or O(ha_vm_monitoring=vmMonitoringOnly).

fields.label: ha vm min up time
fields.description: The number of seconds for the virtual machine's heartbeats to stabilize after the virtual machine has been powered on. Valid only when O(ha_vm_monitoring=vmAndAppMonitoring) or O(ha_vm_monitoring=vmMonitoringOnly). Unit is seconds.

fields.label: ha vm monitoring
fields.description: State of virtual machine health monitoring service. If set to V(vmAndAppMonitoring), HA response to both virtual machine and application heartbeat failure. If set to V(vmMonitoringDisabled), virtual machine health monitoring is disabled. If set to V(vmMonitoringOnly), HA response to virtual machine heartbeat failure. If O(enable=false), then this value is ignored.

fields.label: host isolation response
fields.description: Indicates whether or VMs should be powered off if a host determines that it is isolated from the rest of the compute resource. If set to V(none), do not power off VMs in the event of a host network isolation. If set to V(powerOff), power off VMs in the event of a host network isolation. If set to V(shutdown), shut down VMs guest operating system in the event of a host network isolation.

fields.label: pdl response
fields.description: VM storage protection setting for storage failures categorized as Permenant Device Loss (PDL).

fields.description: Name of the datastore on which backing content library is created. This is required if O(state=present). This parameter is ignored, when O(state=absent). Currently only datastore backing creation is supported.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The hostname or IP address of the vSphere vCenter server. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_HOST) will be used instead.

fields.label: library description
fields.description: The content library description. This is required only if O(state=present). This parameter is ignored, when O(state=absent). Process of updating content library only allows description change.

fields.label: library name
fields.description: The name of VMware content library to manage.

fields.label: library type
fields.description: The content library type. This is required only if O(state=resent). This parameter is ignored, when O(state=absent).

fields.label: proxy host
fields.description: The address of a proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. The format is a hostname or a IP. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_PROXY_HOST) will be used instead.

fields.label: proxy port
fields.description: The port of the HTTP proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_PROXY_PORT) will be used instead.

fields.label: proxy protocol
fields.description: The proxy connection protocol to use. This option is used if the correct proxy protocol cannot be automatically determined.

fields.label: ssl thumbprint
fields.description: The SHA1 SSL thumbprint of the subscribed content library to subscribe to. This is required if O(library_type=subscribed) and the library is https. This parameter is ignored, when O(state=absent). The information can be extracted using openssl using the following example: C(echo | openssl s_client -connect test-library.com:443 |& openssl x509 -fingerprint -noout)

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of content library. If set to V(present) and library does not exists, then content library is created. If set to V(present) and library exists, then content library is updated. If set to V(absent) and library exists, then content library is deleted. If set to V(absent) and library does not exists, no action is taken.

fields.label: subscription url
fields.description: The url of the content library to subscribe to. This is required if O(library_type=subscribed). This parameter is ignored, when O(state=absent).

fields.label: update on demand
fields.description: Whether to download all content on demand. If set to V(true), all content will be downloaded on demand. If set to V(false) content will be downloaded ahead of time. This is required if O(library_type=subscribed). This parameter is ignored, when O(state=absent).

fields.label: advanced settings
fields.description: Define a list of advanced settings to be added to the VMX config. An advanced settings object takes the two fields C(key) and C(value). Incorrect key and values will be ignored.

fields.label: annotation
fields.description: A note or annotation to include in the virtual machine.

fields.label: cluster
fields.description: The cluster name where the virtual machine will run. This is a required parameter, if O(esxi_hostname) is not set. O(esxi_hostname) and O(cluster) are mutually exclusive parameters.

fields.label: convert
fields.description: Specify convert disk type while cloning template or virtual machine.

fields.label: customization spec
fields.description: Unique name identifying the requested customization specification. If set, then overrides O(customization) parameter values.

fields.label: customvalues
fields.description: Define a list of custom values to set on virtual machine. A custom value object takes the two fields C(key) and C(value). Incorrect key and values will be ignored.

fields.label: datacenter
fields.description: Destination datacenter for the deploy operation.

fields.label: datastore
fields.description: Specify datastore or datastore cluster to provision virtual machine. This parameter takes precedence over O(disk[].datastore) parameter. This parameter can be used to override datastore or datastore cluster setting of the virtual machine when deployed from the template. Please see example for more usage.

fields.label: delete from inventory
fields.description: Whether to delete Virtual machine from inventory or delete from disk.

fields.label: esxi hostname
fields.description: The ESXi hostname where the virtual machine will run. This is a required parameter, if O(cluster) is not set. O(esxi_hostname) and O(cluster) are mutually exclusive parameters.

fields.label: folder
fields.description: Destination folder, absolute path to find an existing guest or create the new guest. The folder should include the datacenter. ESXi's datacenter is ha-datacenter. If multiple machines are found with same name, this parameter is used to identify uniqueness of the virtual machine. Examples:    folder: /ha-datacenter/vm    folder: ha-datacenter/vm    folder: /datacenter1/vm    folder: datacenter1/vm    folder: /datacenter1/vm/folder1    folder: datacenter1/vm/folder1    folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm    folder: folder1/datacenter1/vm    folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm/folder2

fields.label: force
fields.description: Ignore warnings and complete the actions. This parameter is useful while removing virtual machine which is powered on state. This module reflects the VMware vCenter API and UI workflow, as such, in some cases the `force` flag will be mandatory to perform the action to ensure you are certain the action has to be taken, no matter what the consequence. This is specifically the case for removing a powered on the virtual machine when O(state=absent).

fields.label: guest id
fields.description: Set the guest ID. This field is required when creating a virtual machine, not required when creating from the template. Valid values are referenced here: U(https://code.vmware.com/apis/358/doc/vim.vm.GuestOsDescriptor.GuestOsIdentifier.html)

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The hostname or IP address of the vSphere vCenter server. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_HOST) will be used instead.

fields.label: is template
fields.description: Flag the instance as a template. This will mark the given virtual machine as template. Note, this may need to be done in a dedicated task invocation that is not making any other changes. For example, user cannot change the state from powered-on to powered-off AND save as template in the same task. See M(community.vmware.vmware_guest) source for more details.

fields.label: linked clone
fields.description: Whether to create a linked clone from the snapshot specified. If specified, then O(snapshot_src) is required parameter.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the virtual machine to work with. Virtual machine names in vCenter are not necessarily unique, which may be problematic, see O(name_match). If multiple virtual machines with same name exists, then O(folder) is required parameter to identify uniqueness of the virtual machine. This parameter is required, if O(state=poweredon), O(state=powered-on), O(state=poweredoff), O(state=powered-off), O(state=present), O(state=restarted), O(state=suspended) and virtual machine does not exists.

fields.label: name match
fields.description: If multiple virtual machines matching the name, use the first or last found.

fields.label: proxy host
fields.description: The address of a proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. The format is a hostname or a IP. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_PROXY_HOST) will be used instead.

fields.label: proxy port
fields.description: The port of the HTTP proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_PROXY_PORT) will be used instead.

fields.label: resource pool
fields.description: Use the given resource pool for virtual machine operation. Resource pool should be child of the selected host parent. When not specified I(Resources) is taken as default value.

fields.label: snapshot src
fields.description: Name of the existing snapshot to use to create a clone of a virtual machine. While creating linked clone using O(linked_clone) parameter, this parameter is required.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Specify the state the virtual machine should be in. If V(present) and virtual machine exists, ensure the virtual machine configurations conforms to task arguments. If V(absent) and virtual machine exists, then the specified virtual machine is removed with it's associated components. If set to one of V(poweredon), V(powered-on), V(poweredoff), V(powered-off), V(present), V(restarted), V(suspended) and virtual machine does not exists, virtual machine is deployed with the given parameters. If set to V(poweredon) or V(powered-on) and virtual machine exists with powerstate other than powered on, then the specified virtual machine is powered on. If set to V(poweredoff) or V(powered-off) and virtual machine exists with powerstate other than powered off, then the specified virtual machine is powered off. If set to V(restarted) and virtual machine exists, then the virtual machine is restarted. If set to V(suspended) and virtual machine exists, then the virtual machine is set to suspended mode. If set to V(shutdownguest) or V(shutdown-guest) and virtual machine exists, then the virtual machine is shutdown. If set to V(rebootguest) or V(reboot-guest) and virtual machine exists, then the virtual machine is rebooted.

fields.label: state change timeout
fields.description: If the O(state=shutdownguest), by default the module will return immediately after sending the shutdown signal. If this argument is set to a positive integer, the module will instead wait for the virtual machine to reach the poweredoff state. The value sets a timeout in seconds for the module to wait for the state change.

fields.label: template
fields.description: Template or existing virtual machine used to create new virtual machine. If this value is not set, virtual machine is created without using a template. If the virtual machine already exists, this parameter will be ignored. From version 2.8 onwards, absolute path to virtual machine or template can be used.

fields.label: use instance uuid
fields.description: Whether to use the VMware instance UUID rather than the BIOS UUID.

fields.label: uuid
fields.description: UUID of the virtual machine to manage if known, this is VMware's unique identifier. This is required if O(name) is not supplied. If virtual machine does not exists, then this parameter is ignored. Please note that a supplied UUID will be ignored on virtual machine creation, as VMware creates the UUID internally.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Allows connection when SSL certificates are not valid. Set to V(false) when certificates are not trusted. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_VALIDATE_CERTS) will be used instead.

fields.label: wait for customization
fields.description: Wait until vCenter detects all guest customizations as successfully completed. When enabled, the VM will automatically be powered on. If vCenter does not detect guest customization start or succeed, failed events after time O(wait_for_customization_timeout) parameter specified, warning message will be printed and task result is fail.

fields.label: wait for customization timeout
fields.description: Define a timeout (in seconds) for the wait_for_customization parameter. Be careful when setting this value since the time guest customization took may differ among guest OSes.

fields.label: wait for ip address
fields.description: Wait until vCenter detects an IP address for the virtual machine. This requires vmware-tools (vmtoolsd) to properly work after creation. vmware-tools needs to be installed on the given virtual machine in order to work with this parameter.

fields.label: wait for ip address timeout
fields.description: Define a timeout (in seconds) for the wait_for_ip_address parameter.

fields.description: If set to V(true) and virtual machine is powered on, it will quiesce the file system in virtual machine. Note that VMware Tools are required for this flag. If virtual machine is powered off or VMware Tools are not available, then this flag is set to V(false). If virtual machine does not provide capability to take quiesce snapshot, then this flag is set to V(false).

fields.label: remove children
fields.description: If set to V(true) and O(state=absent), then entire snapshot subtree is set for removal.

fields.label: snapshot id
fields.description: Sets the snapshot id to manage. This param is available when O(state=absent) or O(state=revert).

fields.label: snapshot name
fields.description: Sets the snapshot name to manage. This param or O(snapshot_id) is required only if O(state) is not C(remove_all)

fields.label: state
fields.description: Manage snapshot(s) attached to a specific virtual machine. If set to V(present) and snapshot absent, then will create a new snapshot with the given name. If set to V(present) and snapshot present, then no changes are made. If set to V(absent) and snapshot present, then snapshot with the given name is removed. If set to V(absent) and snapshot absent, then no changes are made. If set to V(revert) and snapshot present, then virtual machine state is reverted to the given snapshot. If set to V(revert) and snapshot absent, then no changes are made. If set to V(remove_all) and snapshot(s) present, then all snapshot(s) will be removed. If set to V(remove_all) and snapshot(s) absent, then no changes are made.

modules.label: vmware_host
modules.tooltip: Add, remove, or move an ESXi host to, from, or within vCenter

fields.label: add connected
fields.description: If set to V(true), then the host should be connected as soon as it is added. This parameter is ignored if not O(state=present).

fields.label: cluster name
fields.description: Name of the cluster to add the host. If O(folder) is not set, then this parameter is required.

fields.label: datacenter name
fields.description: Name of the datacenter to add the host.

fields.label: esxi hostname
fields.description: ESXi hostname to manage.

fields.label: esxi password
fields.description: ESXi password. Required for adding a host. Optional for reconnect. Unused for removing. No longer a required parameter from version 2.5.

fields.label: esxi ssl thumbprint
fields.description: Specifying the hostsystem certificate's thumbprint. Use following command to get hostsystem certificate's thumbprint -  # openssl x509 -in /etc/vmware/ssl/rui.crt -fingerprint -sha1 -noout

fields.label: esxi username
fields.description: ESXi username. Required for adding a host. Optional for reconnect. If both O(esxi_username) and O(esxi_password) are used Unused for removing. No longer a required parameter from version 2.5.

fields.label: fetch ssl thumbprint
fields.description: Fetch the thumbprint of the host's SSL certificate. This basically disables the host certificate verification (check if it was signed by a recognized CA). Disable this option if you want to allow only hosts with valid certificates to be added to vCenter. If this option is set to V(false) and the certificate can't be verified, an add or reconnect will fail. Unused when O(esxi_ssl_thumbprint) is set. Optional for reconnect, but only used if O(esxi_username) and O(esxi_password) are used. Unused for removing.

fields.label: folder
fields.description: Name of the folder under which host to add. If O(cluster_name) is not set, then this parameter is required. For example, if there is a datacenter 'dc1' under folder called 'Site1' then, this value will be '/Site1/dc1/host'. Here 'host' is an invisible folder under VMware Web Client. Another example, if there is a nested folder structure like '/myhosts/india/pune' under datacenter 'dc2', then O(folder) value will be '/dc2/host/myhosts/india/pune'. Other Examples: '/Site2/dc2/Asia-Cluster/host' or '/dc3/Asia-Cluster/host'

fields.label: force connection
fields.description: Force the connection if the host is already being managed by another vCenter server.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The hostname or IP address of the vSphere vCenter server. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_HOST) will be used instead.

fields.label: proxy host
fields.description: The address of a proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. The format is a hostname or a IP. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_PROXY_HOST) will be used instead.

fields.label: proxy port
fields.description: The port of the HTTP proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_PROXY_PORT) will be used instead.

fields.label: reconnect disconnected
fields.description: Reconnect disconnected hosts. This is only used if O(state=present) and if the host already exists.

fields.label: state
fields.description: If set to V(present), add the host if host is absent. If set to V(present), update the location of the host if host already exists. If set to V(absent), remove the host if host is present. If set to V(absent), do nothing if host already does not exists. If set to V(add_or_reconnect), add the host if it's absent else reconnect it and update the location. If set to V(reconnect), then reconnect the host if it's present and update the location. If set to V(disconnected), disconnect the host if the host already exists.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Allows connection when SSL certificates are not valid. Set to V(false) when certificates are not trusted. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_VALIDATE_CERTS) will be used instead.

fields.label: state

If set to V(vmware_accepted), then accept VIBs that have been accepted by VMware. If set to V(community), then accept all VIBs, even those that are not signed.

fields.label: auto expand
fields.description: Expand a datastore capacity to full if it has free capacity. This parameter can't be extend using another datastore. Can be used to expand a datastore capacity after increasing LUN volume.

fields.label: datastore name
fields.description: Name of the datastore to add/remove.

fields.label: datastore type
fields.description: Type of the datastore to configure (nfs/nfs41/vmfs/vvol).

fields.label: esxi hostname
fields.description: ESXi hostname to manage the datastore. Required when used with a vcenter

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The hostname or IP address of the vSphere vCenter server. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_HOST) will be used instead.

fields.label: nfs path
fields.description: Resource path on NFS host. Required if O(datastore_type=nfs) / O(datastore_type=nfs41) and O(state=present), else unused.

fields.label: nfs ro
fields.description: ReadOnly or ReadWrite mount. Unused if datastore type is not set to O(datastore_type=nfs) / O(datastore_type=nfs41) and not O(state=present).

fields.label: nfs server
fields.description: NFS host serving nfs datastore. Required if O(datastore_type=nfs) / O(datastore_type=nfs41) and O(state=present), else unused. Two or more servers can be defined if O(datastore_type=nfs41)

fields.label: proxy host
fields.description: The address of a proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. The format is a hostname or a IP. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_PROXY_HOST) will be used instead.

fields.label: proxy port
fields.description: The port of the HTTP proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_PROXY_PORT) will be used instead.

fields.label: resignature
fields.description: Allows forcing resignature of unresolved VMFS datastore that already exists on the specified disk device. Unused if not O(datastore_type=vmfs) and not O(state=present).

fields.label: state
fields.description: present: Mount datastore on host if datastore is absent else do nothing. absent: Umount datastore if datastore is present else do nothing.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Allows connection when SSL certificates are not valid. Set to V(false) when certificates are not trusted. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_VALIDATE_CERTS) will be used instead.

fields.label: vasa provider
fields.description: hostname or ipaddress of the VASA provider to use for vVols provisioning

fields.label: vmfs device name
fields.description: Name of the device to be used as VMFS datastore. Required for VMFS datastore type and O(state=present), else unused.

fields.label: vmfs version
fields.description: VMFS version to use for datastore creation. Unused if not O(datastore_type=vmfs) and not O(state=present).

fields.label: destination datastore
fields.description: Name of the destination datastore the virtual machine's vmdk should be moved on.

fields.label: destination datastore cluster
fields.description: Name of the destination datastore cluster (storage pod) the virtual machine's vmdk should be moved on. Only works if drs is enabled for the cluster the vm is running / should run.

fields.label: destination host
fields.description: Name of the destination host the virtual machine should be running on. Version 2.6 onwards, this parameter is not a required parameter, unlike the previous versions.

fields.label: destination resourcepool
fields.description: Name of the destination resource pool where the virtual machine should be running. Resource pool is required if vmotion is done between hosts which are part of different clusters or datacenters. if not passed, resource_pool object will be retrived from host_obj parent.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The hostname or IP address of the vSphere vCenter server. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_HOST) will be used instead.

fields.label: moid
fields.description: Managed Object ID of the instance to manage if known, this is a unique identifier only within a single vCenter instance. This is required if O(vm_name) or O(vm_uuid) is not supplied.

fields.label: proxy host
fields.description: The address of a proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. The format is a hostname or a IP. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_PROXY_HOST) will be used instead.

fields.label: proxy port
fields.description: The port of the HTTP proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_PROXY_PORT) will be used instead.

fields.label: use instance uuid
fields.description: Whether to use the VMware instance UUID rather than the BIOS UUID.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Allows connection when SSL certificates are not valid. Set to V(false) when certificates are not trusted. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_VALIDATE_CERTS) will be used instead.

fields.label: vm name
fields.description: Name of the VM to perform a vMotion on. This is required parameter, if O(vm_uuid) is not set.

fields.label: vm uuid
fields.description: UUID of the virtual machine to perform a vMotion operation on. This is a required parameter, if O(vm_name) or O(moid) is not set.

modules.label: vmware_vspan_session
modules.tooltip: Create or remove a Port Mirroring session.

fields.label: description
fields.description: The description for the session.

fields.label: destination port
fields.description: Destination port that received the mirrored packets. Also any port designated in the value of this property can not match the source port in any of the Distributed Port Mirroring session.

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Whether the session is enabled.

fields.label: encapsulation vlan id
fields.description: VLAN ID used to encapsulate the mirrored traffic.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The hostname or IP address of the vSphere vCenter server. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_HOST) will be used instead.

fields.label: mirrored packet length
fields.description: An integer that describes how much of each frame to mirror. If unset, all of the frame would be mirrored. Setting this property to a smaller value is useful when the consumer will look only at the headers. The value cannot be less than 60.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the session.

fields.label: normal traffic allowed
fields.description: Whether or not destination ports can send and receive "normal" traffic. Setting this to false will make mirror ports be used solely for mirroring and not double as normal access ports.

fields.label: proxy host
fields.description: The address of a proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. The format is a hostname or a IP. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_PROXY_HOST) will be used instead.

fields.label: proxy port
fields.description: The port of the HTTP proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_PROXY_PORT) will be used instead.

fields.label: sampling rate
fields.description: Sampling rate of the session. If its value is n, one of every n packets is mirrored. Valid values are between 1 to 65535.

fields.label: session type
fields.description: Select the mirroring type. In V(encapsulatedRemoteMirrorSource) session, Distributed Ports can be used as source entities, and IP address can be used as destination entities. In V(remoteMirrorDest) session, VLAN IDs can be used as source entities, and Distributed Ports can be used as destination entities. In V(remoteMirrorSource) session, Distributed Ports can be used as source entities, and uplink ports name can be used as destination entities. In V(dvPortMirror) session, Distributed Ports can be used as both source and destination entities.

fields.label: source port received
fields.description: Source port for which received packets are mirrored.

fields.label: source port transmitted
fields.description: Source port for which transmitted packets are mirrored.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Create or remove the session.

fields.label: strip original vlan
fields.description: Whether to strip the original VLAN tag. If false, the original VLAN tag will be preserved on the mirrored traffic. If O(encapsulation_vlan_id) has been set and this property is V(false), the frames will be double tagged with the original VLAN ID as the inner tag.

fields.label: switch
fields.description: The name of the distributed vSwitch on which to add or remove the mirroring session.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Allows connection when SSL certificates are not valid. Set to V(false) when certificates are not trusted. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(VMWARE_VALIDATE_CERTS) will be used instead.
